name: Buffer Overflow Exploit & Defense
description: A buffer overflow occurs when unchecked input is copied past a buffer's bounds, overwriting the return address to redirect control flow to attacker code; modern defenses such as stack canaries, ASLR, and non-executable memory detect or block the attack, while proper bounds checking rejects the input outright.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Program reads input"]
    N2["Copy to fixed buffer"]
    N3{"Bounds checked?"}
    N4["Reject oversized input"]
    N5["Overflow buffer"]
    N6["Overwrite return addr"]
    N7{"Defenses enabled?"}
    N8["Stack canary detects"]
    N9["ASLR + NX block"]
    N10["Abort safely"]
    N11["Hijack control flow"]
    N12["Prevented vs success"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|yes| N4
    N4 --> N12
    N3 -->|no| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N12
    N7 -->|no| N11
    N11 --> N12

sources.title: Smashing the stack for fun and profit
StackGuard: automatic adaptive detection and prevention of buffer-overflow attacks
sources.authors: Cowan, C.; Pu, C.; Maier, D.; et al.
